results.summary: The Minister for Foreign Affairs and Senior Minister of State for Foreign Affairs addressed the question regarding Singapore's position on recognizing the Palestinian state in their Ministerial Statements on the Situation in the Middle East delivered on 22 September 2025. The Government's current stance remains unchanged despite declarations by Australia and key G7 members to recognize the state at the next UN General Assembly. Factors considered for sovereign statehood recognition in accordance with international law were outlined in those statements, which supersede this specific oral question. No new policy measures or revised timelines were announced during this session.

results.title: SkillsFuture Credit Usage towards Professional Licensure or Examination Fees
results.summary: The SkillsFuture Credit (Mid-Career) of $4,000 is restricted to training fees and cannot offset professional licensure or examination fees, as the policy prioritizes substantive training for mid-career Singaporeans. While the base tier currently covers approximately 360 industry-recognised examination-only certifications supported by relevant public agencies, the Ministry will continue reviewing how the credit supports industry-relevant training. The Minister declined to confirm a pilot for a capped, outcomes-based list of employability-critical exams eligible for the credit at this time.

results.title: Statistics on Ridership and Average Load Factors for City Direct Services 675, 676 and 677 since January 2025 and During Peak Hours
results.summary: Acting Minister for Transport Jeffrey Siow stated that loading for City Direct Services 675, 676, and 677 has stabilised since their January 2025 launch. Current average load factors are approximately 35% to 45% during morning peak hours and 10% to 15% during evening peak hours. Specific ridership statistics since January 2025 were not provided in the response. The Minister did not elaborate on future adjustments or capacity constraints for these services.

results.title: Statistics on Students who are Unsuccessful in Their Applications in Joint Admission and Direct Admission Exercises
results.summary: Over the past five years, ITE graduates have maintained strong progression rates to polytechnics via three routes: the Joint Polytechnic Admissions Exercise (JPAE), Early Admissions Exercise (EAE), and Direct Admissions Exercise (DAE). Statistics show that one in two applicants received offers through JPAE, one in three through EAE, and approximately one in ten through DAE. This year, roughly 5,800 ITE graduates secured full-time polytechnic diplomas or ITE diploma programmes, representing nearly half of the graduating cohort. The Government confirmed it will continue supporting these upgrading aspirations.

results.title: Statistics on Teachers Who Joined Flexi-adjunct Teaching Scheme and Median Duration of Teachers on Scheme
results.summary: From 2022 to 2024, an average of approximately 220 teachers joined the Flexi-Adjunct Teaching Scheme within 12 months of leaving the Education Service annually. The Minister clarified that because the scheme operates as casual employment, tracking the median duration teachers remain on it is not meaningful. Consequently, no specific duration statistics were provided for these years.

results.title: Strategies to Safeguard Job Stability for Singaporeans in Light of Trade Tensions and Structural Shifts in Labour Market
results.summary: The Minister outlined a three-pronged strategy to safeguard job stability by facilitating workforce transformation rather than saving unproductive roles. First, over $600 million was allocated in the current Budget for the Enterprise Workforce Transformation Package and NTUC's Company Training Committee Grant to support business upgrades. Second, more than $10 billion has been spent over five years on local initiatives like the Career Conversion Programmes and SkillsFuture schemes to equip workers with future-ready skills. Third, new measures include the Career Health SG initiative, a Tripartite Workgroup on Senior Employment, and the SkillsFuture Jobseeker Support scheme for involuntarily displaced workers. The Singapore Economic Resilience Taskforce will monitor global trade impacts, while the Economic Strategy Review charts a forward-looking blueprint to secure opportunities for businesses and workers.

results.title: Strengthening Financial Governance, Procurement Practices and IT Controls across Government and Addressing Recurring Lapses
results.summary: The Government has implemented systemic measures to strengthen financial governance, procurement practices, and IT controls across Ministries and statutory boards in response to the Auditor-General's Report FY2024/2025. Key actions include updating training programmes to equip officers with skills in threat modelling, privileged account management, and contract risk evaluation, alongside stricter IT control requirements for high-impact systems. Agencies are mandated to regularly assess system risks, rectify identified gaps, and tighten internal controls under GovTech guidance. Internal auditors will track corrective actions and share improvement areas across the public sector to ensure recurring lapses are addressed and public resources are managed responsibly.

results.title: Strengthening Singapore’s Cyber Defence
results.summary: The Singapore Armed Forces' Digital and Intelligence Service (DIS) inaugurated the Defence Cyber Command (DCCOM) earlier this year to consolidate cybersecurity entities and strengthen capabilities. DCCOM serves as a focal point for deepening partnerships with the Cyber Security Agency of Singapore (CSA), other government agencies, and critical information infrastructure owners regarding operations, training, and technical expertise sharing. To expand cyber talent, the DIS collaborates with schools via the Sentinel Programme and introduced a Cyber Work-Learn Scheme with polytechnics and universities, allowing students to undertake advanced operational cyber roles during National Service or Regular Service. Additionally, Operationally Ready National Servicemen with relevant skills can be redeployed to the DIS, with plans to continue expanding the number of contribution pathways for National Servicemen.

results.title: Stronger Penalties for Importers and Distributors of Vapes
results.summary: The Ministry of Health is reviewing legislation to align penalties for vape importers and distributors with those for etomidate-laced vapes, following findings that one in three confiscated devices tested positive for the substance. This review aims to address the risk of using vapes to deliver unknown drugs. The new legislation, which will cover penalties for all e-vaporiser suppliers, is planned for enactment in the first quarter of 2026. The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health confirmed this timeline during the parliamentary session.

results.title: Supporting SMEs in Navigating Overseas Regulatory Challenges and Geopolitical Risks
results.summary: The Government announced the permanent doubling of the maximum trade loan quantum under the Enterprise Financing Scheme to $10 million and extended the Double Tax Deduction for Internationalisation by five years. Additionally, the $100,000 grant cap for new markets under the Market Readiness Assistance scheme was extended by a year. EnterpriseSG operates over 35 overseas centres providing market intelligence and partner introductions, while the GlobalConnect programme offers advisory services and business trips via the Singapore Business Federation. To address external uncertainties, the Centre for the Future of Trade and Investment was launched in July 2024 to advise on tariff and Free Trade Agreement queries. Furthermore, the Business Adaptation Grant is scheduled for October 2025 to assist eligible firms impacted by United States trade tariffs in adapting operations and strengthening supply chain resilience.

results.title: Take-up Rate and Assessed Outcomes for Household Services Scheme
results.summary: The Household Services Scheme (HSS), formalised in 2021, enables companies to hire migrant workers for part-time domestic services like cleaning and pet-sitting, with participating firms rising from 80 to 240. The Ministry of Manpower supports HSS through additional work permit quotas and source country concessions but maintains that market forces determine pricing, citing sufficient consumer choice and no current need for intervention. While the Ministry recognises efficiency gains from workers serving multiple households, it does not allow migrant domestic workers to concurrently participate in HSS. This restriction prevents liability ambiguity in case of injuries and avoids illegal moonlighting that could undermine the work pass framework and harm law-abiding businesses.

results.title: Taxi and Private Hire Car Drivers Turning 75 from 2022 to 2024 and Maintaining Vocational Licence Age Limit
results.summary: The Ministry provided data on taxi and private hire car (PHC) drivers turning 75 from 2022 to 2024, noting that the maximum vocational licence age limit remains 75 years. This limit was established after reviewing past accident statistics and consulting the Singapore Medical Association to ensure driver fitness and commuter safety. The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that maintaining this age constraint is essential for road safety, as drivers must remain fit to operate vehicles. No specific figures for the number of drivers turning 75 in each year were included in the provided text.

results.title: The Future of Library
results.summary: The National Library Board (NLB) reports that while physical book reading among adults dropped to 75% in 2024, digital consumption rose to 14.8 million borrowed items, with total library visitorship reaching 20.8 million. To adapt, NLB is executing the Libraries of the Future Masterplan (2017–2030) to revamp older facilities and relocate libraries to accessible transport hubs, citing a $22 million budget saving for the Geylang East Library relocation to Tanjong Katong Complex. The agency continues to strengthen its mandate as a community node through initiatives like THRIVE@libraries for adults, StoryGen for AI exploration, and the kidsREAD@Home programme for lower-income families. Additionally, NLB has launched services for persons with disabilities at Punggol Library and expanded digital safety resources via the S.U.R.E. campaign to support citizens in a rapidly evolving digital landscape.

results.title: Time Limit for Minister's Speech
results.summary: Members granted consent to exempt the proceedings from Standing Order No 48(8), removing the time limit for Minister Ong Ye Kung's upcoming speech. The Leader of the House moved this request, and the Deputy Speaker approved the exemption before the Minister was scheduled to speak. No specific duration or alternative time cap was established for the speech in this transcript.

results.title: Total Quantum of Government Grants and Subsidies Provided to Operators or Preschools Under AOP and POP
results.summary: Government expenditure on early childhood rose from $1.5 billion in FY2020 to over $2 billion in FY2024, increasing average spending per preschool-aged child from $7,900 to $13,000. Funding for Anchor and Partner Operator (AOP and POP) preschools supports quality improvements and adherence to monthly fee caps, which were lowered to $640 and $680 respectively for full-day childcare this year. These caps will further decrease to $610 and $650 in 2026, reducing costs for families alongside basic and means-tested subsidies. The Minister did not provide specific grant quantum data for the last five years or per-enrolled-student figures for each scheme.

results.title: Understating Gini Coefficient and Under-accounting of Income Inequality due to Exclusion of Asset and Investment Income
results.summary: The Ministry of Finance and Department of Statistics acknowledge that non-employment income from assets and investments is often under-reported, which may understate Singapore's Gini coefficient and income inequality. While better-off households typically hold more such assets, the Ministry is currently studying improved measurement methods for this income type. Findings regarding these measurement enhancements will be shared when ready. The response does not provide a specific timeline for when these findings will be released.

results.title: Update on Considerations for Default Publication of Anonymised Employment Claims Tribunal Decisions
results.summary: The Executive maintains that Employment Claims Tribunal judgments should not be published by default, leaving publication decisions to the Courts. However, the Judiciary will proactively publish a curated selection of anonymised judgments starting in the second half of 2026 to enhance public understanding of employer-employee legal rights and responsibilities. This phased release aims to balance transparency with the need for timely justice and procedural confidentiality. Parties may also request access to specific judgments with Court approval. This approach replaces a potential default publication policy with a selective strategy to improve legal awareness while preserving the confidential nature of individual proceedings.

results.title: Update on Implementation of ERP 2.0 and Plans to Ensure Transparency and Data Privacy for Users
results.summary: As of 23 September 2025, over 750,000 vehicles (75%) have installed ERP 2.0 On-Board Units, with full installation targeted for 2026. The system delivers real-time traffic updates, including flood warnings and bus lane alerts, alongside estimated travel times and carpark availability. Since 1 September 2025, motorists can conveniently pay missed ERP charges via the OBU. The Land Transport Authority (LTA) enforces strict data safeguards, utilizing only anonymised or aggregated data for traffic management and planning while adhering to Government-wide data security standards and strict guidelines on inter-agency data sharing.

results.title: Update on Pigeon Management Plan Expansion to Address Roosting and Hygiene Issues
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the response regarding plans to expand the Pigeon Management Plan to additional constituencies and HDB blocks has already been provided via an oral reply to Question No 103 on the Order Paper dated 22 September 2025. No new measures, timelines, or specific eligibility criteria for this expansion were announced during this session. The parliamentary record indicates the matter was previously addressed rather than introducing fresh policy signals or operational constraints at this time.

results.title: Update on Plans and Justification to Terminate Allied Educators Programme
results.summary: The Minister for Education confirmed no plans to terminate the Allied Educators programme entirely, noting these staff are integral to the teaching force. MOE is reshaping the workforce by growing Special Education Needs Officers and School Counsellors while scaling back Allied Educators in Teaching and Learning. Over the past nine years, the ministry has facilitated transitions for these educators into roles of greater need through work attachments and training, with most moving into alternative careers within MOE and the Public Service.

results.title: Update on Review of Practices and Safeguards on Sinkhole Incident on 26 July 2025
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated that the inquiry regarding the 26 July 2025 sinkhole incident at Tanjong Katong Road South was previously addressed in a written answer on 22 September 2025. Users are directed to consult the official response recorded in the 22 September 2025 Order Paper for details on the review timeline, sewer shaft inspection frequencies, and additional worker safety measures. No new information or updates were provided during this session.

results.title: Updated Risk Analysis on Impacts of Sumatran Earthquakes and Mersing Fault Zone and Enhancing Structural Safety of HDB Buildings
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that all HDB blocks are designed to withstand regional seismic forces, and recent tremor sensor data from HDB and BCA showed the Johor earthquake had no impact on Singapore buildings. While risk assessment frameworks will be reviewed as necessary, the Building and Construction Authority maintains a rigorous regime of periodic structural inspections to identify defects. Real-time monitoring continues for selected buildings to assess potential impacts from future seismic activities in the region.

results.title: Visitors Demographic Profiles for Kallang Basin Swimming Complex and St Wilfred Sport Centre Over Past 10 Years
results.summary: Over the past five years, Kallang Basin Swimming Complex recorded an average annual non-unique visitorship of approximately 52,000, with roughly half aged 20 to 59. During the same period, St Wilfred Sport Centre saw an average annual non-unique visitorship of about 124,000, where slightly more than 80% of visitors fell within the 20 to 59 age range. The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth provided these demographic profiles, noting that specific data on sex and race was not detailed in the response.

results.title: Volumes at Khatib MRT Station's Pedestrian Overhead Bridge and Possible Expansion to Accommodate Increased Usage
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) proactively monitors pedestrian volumes and implements infrastructure upgrades as needed. In early 2025, LTA reviewed traffic flow at Khatib MRT station's overhead bridge and concluded its current width can accommodate existing and projected footfall, including anticipated increases from new BTO flats in the nearby Chencharu estate. No expansion is currently warranted based on this assessment.

results.title: Waiving Non-selection Counts when Final BTO Unit Price Exceeds Indicative Price Range
results.summary: HDB maintains that all Build-To-Order (BTO) flat prices have remained within the provided indicative ranges to date, though it retains flexibility to waive non-selection counts (NSC) under specific conditions. Waivers are granted when applicants face limited choices, such as having 10 or fewer flats available in a BTO exercise or five or fewer in a Sale of Balance Flats exercise. Additionally, HDB assesses appeals on a case-by-case basis for applicants facing extenuating circumstances that impact their ability to proceed with a purchase. The Board states that these measures ensure efficient flat allocation, prioritizing those with urgent housing needs while allowing informed decision-making through published price ranges.

results.title: Work-related Road Injuries and Fatalities in 1H2025
results.summary: Data on 2025 work-related road injuries will be released by the Ministry of Manpower next year, while 2024 figures recorded four fatalities and 20 major injuries. All lorries weighing 3,500kg to 12,000kg must install speed limiters by deadlines ranging from 1 January 2026 to 1 July 2027 based on registration and weight. Non-compliant owners face fines up to $1,000, failed inspections, and driving prohibitions. The Traffic Police incentivizes early installation by prioritizing drivers for Class 4 lessons if their fleet complies. Safety improvements also include facilitating worker dormitory co-location near workplaces like shipyards and encouraging industry associations to transport workers via buses instead of lorries. Additional protective measures for workers transported in lorries have been rolled out over recent years.

results.title: President's Speech
results.summary: The President outlined a new national strategy built on three pillars—security, investment, and trust—to navigate geopolitical shifts, AI disruption, and demographic challenges. Singapore will maintain foreign policy continuity while diversifying partnerships beyond traditional allies to include the Global South, ensuring resilience against hybrid threats like cyberattacks and cheap drones. Domestically, the government aims to update social safety nets to be portable and preventive rather than charitable, addressing the inadequacy of current schemes like the Jobseeker Support Scheme for median income earners. Concurrently, efforts focus on strengthening SMEs, which contribute 50% of GDP and employ nearly 70% of the workforce, through structural reforms and targeted relief to foster inclusive growth and protect the middle class from economic squeeze.
